title: Spicy Fluffy Middle Eastern Lamb Flatbread
description: A Whole30-friendly, spicy, and fluffy Middle Eastern-inspired dish featuring fresh thyme, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This recipe combines the warmth of lamb with the comfort of a homemade flatbread.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine almond flour, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper.
Add the coconut oil, eggs, and water to the bowl. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a separate pan, cook the lamb mince over medium heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped fresh thyme to the lamb and stir to combine.
Place a spoonful of the lamb mixture onto one half of each flatbread circle.
Fold the flatbread in half to enclose the filling and press the edges to seal.
Place the flatbreads on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
